briefdescription.para: The data type (and object index) of a boolean truth value.
The data type (and object index) of an 8-bit signed integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 16-bit signed integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 32-bit signed integer.
The data type (and object index) of an 8-bit unsigned integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 16-bit unsigned integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 32-bit unsigned integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 32-bit IEEE-754 floating-point number.
The data type (and object index) of an array of visible characters.
The data type (and object index) of an array of octets.
The data type (and object index) of an array of (16-bit) Unicode characters.
The data type (and object index) of a 48-bit structure representing the absolute time.
The data type (and object index) of a 48-bit structure representing a time difference.
The data type (and object index) of a 56-bit structure representing Spacecraft Elapsed Time (SCET).

detaileddescription.para: The data type (and object index) of a 64-bit structure representing Spacecraft Universal Time Coordinated (SUTC). The data type (and object index) of an arbitrary large block of data.

briefdescription.para: The data type (and object index) of a 24-bit signed integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 64-bit IEEE-754 floating-point number.
The data type (and object index) of a 40-bit signed integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 48-bit signed integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 56-bit signed integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 64-bit signed integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 24-bit unsigned integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 40-bit unsigned integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 48-bit unsigned integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 56-bit unsigned integer.
The data type (and object index) of a 64-bit unsigned integer.
Returns true if the specified (static) data type is a basic type, and false if not.
Returns true if the specified (static) data type is an array, and false if not.
Returns the native size (in bytes) of a value of the specified data type, or 1 if it is not a static data type.

detaileddescription.para: In case of strings or domains, this function returns the size of a pointer.

briefdescription.para: Returns the alignment requirements (in bytes) of a value of the specified data type, or 1 if it is not a static data type.

detaileddescription.para: In case of strings or domains, this function returns the alignment requirements of a pointer.

briefdescription.para: This header file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the CANopen type definitions.

detaileddescription.para:
